title: Selenicereus minutiflorus
taxonomy: Selenicereus minutiflorus belongs to the family Cactaceae. This species is further classified into the subfamily Cactoideae and the tribe Hylocereeae. It is a species of cactus that is native to Mexico and parts of Central America. Selenicereus minutiflorus is a climbing cactus with small, funnel-shaped flowers. The species is closely related to other cacti in the Selenicereus genus, which are known for their fragrant and showy flowers.

side_effects: Selenicereus minutiflorus is a species of cactus, and like many plants in this genus, it may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals due to the presence of spines and sap. intro: Selenicereus minutiflorus, also known as the dwarf night-blooming cereus, is a species of flowering cactus native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation and pain, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Selenicereus minutiflorus include flavonoids, glycosides, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Selenicereus minutiflorus is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to alleviate pain and reduce inflammation.
